text.title: User Interview Frameworks and Structure
text.summary: Two interview frameworks (basic user-interview and customer-interview) with typed stage structures, question categories, and golden rules.

section.title: Interview Types
section.p: Choose type based on validation stage:

rule.statement: Match interview type to validation stage: Problem interviews in early discovery, Solution interviews after the problem is confirmed, Usability interviews when a prototype exists.
rule.rationale: Running a usability interview without a validated problem wastes cycles; running a problem interview with a prototype primes the user to evaluate your solution rather than describe their reality.

section.p: Problem interviews: open-ended exploration, goal is to confirm problem existence. Solution interviews: prototype feedback, goal is to test solution fit. Usability interviews: task-based, goal is to identify UX friction. Discovery interviews: 30 min, context-setting before a deep problem interview.

section.title: 45-Minute Interview Structure

rule.statement: Run a 45-minute interview in five timed stages: Warm-up (5 min), Current State (10 min), Pain Exploration (15 min), Solution Probing (10 min), Wrap-up (5 min).
rule.rationale: Timed stages force the researcher to protect the deep-dive phase; without structure, warm-up expands and pain exploration shrinks.

section.p: Warm-up: build rapport, no sales. Current State: how do they do it today, what tools. Pain Exploration: what is frustrating, past behavior, workarounds. Solution Probing: show concept, get reaction, willingness to pay. Wrap-up: referrals, follow-up permission.

section.title: Golden Rules

rule.statement: Talk less than 20% of the time; never pitch; ask about past behavior not future intent; look for emotional signals (frustration, excitement).
rule.rationale: Future-intent questions ("would you use this?") produce socially desirable answers, not behavioral data. Past behavior is the only reliable signal of what users actually do.

section.p: Sample past-behavior questions: "Tell me about the last time you [problem]..." / "What did you try? What happened?" / "On a scale of 1-10, how frustrating is this?" / "How much time or money does this cost you?"

section.title: Question Types

rule.statement: Use four question types in sequence: Open (explore), Probing (go deeper), Clarifying (get example), Summary (confirm understanding).
rule.rationale: Open questions surface the topic; probing questions reveal intensity; clarifying questions get specifics; summary confirms you understood correctly before moving on.

section.p: Open: "Tell me about..." Probing: "Why is that?" Clarifying: "Can you give an example?" Summary: "So you are saying..."

text.reference: Full typed guide with hypothesis, script, and notes table
